questions.stem: Lily ______ the novel last month, and she ______ it three times so far.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。last month（上个月）是具体的过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时bought；so far（到目前为止）是现在完成时的标志词，且three times表示动作重复的次数，用现在完成时has read。B选项has bought错误，因为buy是瞬间动词，不能与last month连用表示过去购买的动作；C选项第二个read缺少完成时态；D选项两个都用完成时，但bought不能用完成时与last month搭配。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The student ______ won the English speech contest is from Class 3.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词是The student（指人），且关系代词在从句中作主语（won的主语），因此用who。B选项which用于指物；C选项whom用于指人但作宾语；D选项whose表示所属关系（谁的）。口诀：先行词指人作主语用who，作宾语用whom/who，指物用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new bridge near our school ______ by the workers last year.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查一般过去时的被动语态。last year（去年）表明是过去时间，bridge（桥）是被建造的，因此用一般过去时的被动语态was built（主语是单数）。A选项is built是一般现在时被动，时态不符；C选项built是主动语态过去式，但bridge不能自己建造自己；D选项builds是主动语态第三人称单数，同样逻辑错误。被动语态结构：be + 过去分词，注意时态变化。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Could you please tell me ______? I'd like to send a package.
options: A. where is the nearest post office
B. where the nearest post office is
C. where was the nearest post office
D. where does the nearest post office
questions.explanation: 这道题考查宾语从句的语序。宾语从句必须使用陈述语序（主语+谓语），不能用疑问语序。A和D都是疑问语序（is/does在主语前），C也是疑问语序且时态错误（was）。B选项where the nearest post office is是陈述语序（the nearest post office是主语，is是谓语），且时态与主句Could you...（委婉请求，实际是现在时）相符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old photos bring back sweet ______ to my grandmother.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查名词复数及词义辨析。photos是复数，对应的回忆也应该是复数memories（memories是memory的复数形式）。moment表示时刻，不符合句意（照片带回的是回忆，不是时刻）。A和C是单数形式，与these old photos不匹配。固定搭配bring back memories（勾起回忆）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: Please ______ the lights when you leave the classroom to save electricity.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查动词短语辨析。turn off表示关闭（电器、水源等），符合句意离开教室关灯。turn on是打开；turn up是调大（音量）或出现；turn down是调小（音量）或拒绝。根据save electricity（节约用电）的语境，应该是关灯。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It is ______ to play computer games for five hours without a break. (health)
questions.explanation: 这道题考查词性转换和构词法。It is + 形容词 + to do sth.是固定句型，需要形容词。health是名词；unhealthily是副词；healthy是形容词健康的，但句意五小时不休息玩游戏应该是不健康的，因此用unhealthy（un-前缀表示否定）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school launched an innovative environmental protection project named "Zero Waste Classroom". The primary objective was to significantly reduce daily waste production and help students develop awareness of environmental sustainability through hands-on practical activities.

Every student was required to bring reusable lunch boxes, metal spoons, and personal water bottles instead of relying on disposable plastic containers. At first, some students found it (8)______ to consistently remember bringing their own tableware every morning. They frequently left them at home and had to awkwardly borrow from classmates. However, after persisting diligently for approximately two weeks, this practice gradually transformed into a beneficial daily (9)______ that contributed positively to both personal health and environmental conservation.

The highlight of the entire project was undoubtedly the intense inter-class recycling competition held during the final week. All students enthusiastically participated by collecting various recyclable materials including plastic bottles, cardboard, and waste paper. Through collective teamwork and dedication, my class successfully gathered over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials, (10)______ ultimately won us the prestigious first prize and a beautiful "Green Class" banner.

Through this deeply meaningful project, we truly understood that seemingly small daily actions can indeed make a big (11)______ to the health of our planet. Nowadays, I consistently carry an eco-friendly cloth bag when going shopping and strictly refuse all forms of single-use plastics. I firmly believe that if every individual tries their best to protect our precious environment, we will undoubtedly enjoy a (12)______ and more sustainable future for generations to come.
questions.explanation: 根据上下文语境，后文提到"They frequently left them at home"（他们经常忘在家里），说明一开始记住带餐具是困难的，因此选difficult。A选项easy与后文矛盾；C选项interesting和D选项boring在文中没有情感线索支持。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 考查固定搭配和语境理解。前文提到"persisting diligently for approximately two weeks"（坚持两周），形成了习惯，habit（习惯）符合语境。hobby是爱好（用于娱乐），rule是规则，game是游戏。daily habit是固定搭配，表示日常习惯。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 考查非限制性定语从句。此处修饰前面整个句子"my class successfully gathered over 200 kilograms of recyclable materials"，且从句前有逗号隔开，是非限制性定语从句，用which引导（指代前面整件事）。that不能用于非限制性定语从句；what不引导定语从句；who指人。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 考查固定短语。make a difference是固定搭配，表示有影响、起作用。句意：小小的日常行动确实能对地球健康产生重大影响。make a decision（做决定）、make a mistake（犯错误）、make progress（取得进步）均不符合句意。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 考查形容词比较级和语境逻辑。前文提到"if everyone tries their best to protect our precious environment"（如果每个人尽力保护环境），结果应该是更好（better）的未来。worse（更糟）与文意相反；busier（更忙）和quieter（更安静）与环保主题关联不大。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Many teenagers today struggle to maintain healthy sleep habits due to heavy academic pressure and the temptation of digital devices. However, scientific research consistently demonstrates that adequate sleep is absolutely crucial for adolescent development and academic performance. A comprehensive study conducted by the National Sleep Foundation reveals that students who regularly sleep less than seven hours per night frequently experience significant difficulty concentrating during morning classes and show reduced problem-solving abilities.

Dr. Li Ming, a renowned sleep specialist at Beijing Hospital, provides an interesting explanation regarding memory consolidation. He describes the sleeping brain as a sophisticated computer system that systematically processes and stores information absorbed throughout the day. "Think of sleep as pressing the save button on your computer," he illustrates. "Without sufficient high-quality sleep, students may unfortunately forget approximately forty percent of the knowledge they studied previously, regardless of how hard they worked."

However, achieving good sleep involves more than merely spending long hours in bed. The actual quality of sleep matters tremendously and depends on multiple environmental and behavioral factors. Experts recommend maintaining bedrooms at cool temperatures with minimal lighting, strictly avoiding electronic screens for at least one hour before bedtime, and eliminating caffeine consumption after four o'clock in the afternoon. These practices help the brain produce melatonin naturally.

Interestingly, the article also mentions that incorporating a short power nap after lunch can significantly enhance memory retention and afternoon alertness. But timing is essential—ideal naps should last between twenty to thirty minutes maximum. Sleeping longer during daytime hours might actually leave you feeling groggy (昏昏沉沉的) and disoriented rather than refreshed and energized.
questions.stem: According to the passage, students who don't get enough sleep may forget about ______ of what they studied.
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第二段最后一句"students may unfortunately forget approximately forty percent of the knowledge they studied previously"（学生可能会忘记大约40%之前学过的知识），明确提到40%。解题技巧：细节题要找准关键词（forget/percent），回原文定位具体数字。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. Students should completely avoid using digital devices.
B. Taking long naps during the day is beneficial for health.
C. Sleep quality is equally important as sleep quantity.
D. Teenagers need less sleep than adults to function well.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第三段第一、二句"achieving good sleep involves more than merely spending long hours in bed. The actual quality of sleep matters tremendously"（获得良好睡眠不仅仅是长时间躺在床上，睡眠质量同样极其重要），可推断出睡眠质量与睡眠时间同等重要。A选项completely avoid过于绝对；B选项与最后一段"Sleeping longer during daytime hours might actually leave you feeling groggy"矛盾；D选项文中未提及且与科学常识相反。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "crucial" in Paragraph 1 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文，后文提到睡眠不足会导致注意力不集中、解决问题能力下降，且第二段详细说明睡眠对记忆巩固的作用，因此crucial应表示重要的（important）。difficult（困难的）、boring（无聊的）、dangerous（危险的）均不符合语境。解题技巧：通过下文对睡眠重要性的详细阐述来反推词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Use Computers Wisely
B. The Importance of Sleep for Teenagers
C. Ways to Improve Memory Quickly
D. Why We Should Avoid Caffeine
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章首段提出睡眠对青少年至关重要，第二段讲睡眠与记忆的关系，第三段讲睡眠质量，第四段讲午睡，全文围绕青少年睡眠的重要性展开。A选项只涉及第二段的比喻；C选项只是睡眠作用的一部分；D选项只是第三段的细节。解题技巧：最佳标题应涵盖全文主要内容，而不是某个细节。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Joining extracurricular clubs represents an excellent opportunity to enrich your middle school experience and develop new friendships. However, many eighth-grade students often feel genuinely confused about maintaining an appropriate balance between club activities and academic responsibilities. Here are several practical strategies to help you navigate this challenge successfully.

(17)______ Before committing to any specific organization, take time to reflect deeply on your genuine personal interests and passions. If you possess a strong love for music and singing, joining the school choir might prove absolutely perfect for you. Conversely, for those fascinated by science and technology, the robotics club would likely be an ideal choice matching your curiosity.

Next, develop effective time management skills by creating a structured weekly schedule and adhering to it consistently. (18)______ Always remember that your academic performance and schoolwork should permanently remain your top priority throughout the semester.

(19)______ Never hesitate to approach senior students or club leaders for valuable practical advice. These experienced individuals have successfully navigated the challenges of balancing studies with activities. Additionally, you can always consult with your teachers or school counselor if you ever feel overwhelmed by competing demands.

Finally, cultivate the ability to decline additional commitments when necessary. (20)______ It is completely acceptable to occasionally skip a non-essential club meeting when facing important examinations or deadlines. Ultimately, the quality of your participation matters significantly more than the sheer quantity of clubs listed on your resume.
options: A. However, don't let hobbies affect your grades negatively.
B. First, choose clubs based on your personal interests.
C. As a result, you will become popular in school quickly.
D. Then, seek help from experienced senior students.
E. On the other hand, taking part in too many clubs is tiring.
questions.explanation: 考查段落衔接和逻辑顺序。后文出现"Before committing to any specific organization"（在加入任何组织之前），且提到根据兴趣选择（music/robotics），且下一段开头是Next，因此此处应是First引出的第一条建议。B选项First, choose clubs based on your personal interests（首先，基于个人兴趣选择社团）符合逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, don't let hobbies affect your grades negatively.
B. First, choose clubs based on your personal interests.
C. As a result, you will become popular in school quickly.
D. Then, seek help from experienced senior students.
E. On the other hand, taking part in too many clubs is tiring.
questions.explanation: 考查转折关系。前文讲制定时间表坚持执行，后文讲"学业应该永远是首要任务"，中间需要转折过渡，提醒不要因为社团影响成绩。A选项However, don't let hobbies affect your grades negatively（然而，不要让爱好负面影响你的成绩）起到转折提醒作用，连接前后逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, don't let hobbies affect your grades negatively.
B. First, choose clubs based on your personal interests.
C. As a result, you will become popular in school quickly.
D. Then, seek help from experienced senior students.
E. On the other hand, taking part in too many clubs is tiring.
questions.explanation: 考查段落主题句和顺序衔接。前文有First... Next...，此处应是Then...（然后/接下来）引出的第三条建议。后文提到"approach senior students or club leaders for advice"（向学长或社团领导寻求建议），与D选项Then, seek help from experienced senior students（然后，向有经验的学长寻求帮助）完全对应。
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, don't let hobbies affect your grades negatively.
B. First, choose clubs based on your personal interests.
C. As a result, you will become popular in school quickly.
D. Then, seek help from experienced senior students.
E. On the other hand, taking part in too many clubs is tiring.
questions.explanation: 考查解释说明关系。前文提到"learn to say no when necessary"（必要时学会拒绝），后文解释偶尔跳过不重要的会议是可以的，因此中间需要说明为什么要拒绝/说不。E选项On the other hand, taking part in too many clubs is tiring（另一方面，参加太多社团很累）解释了为什么要学会拒绝，因为太多社团会让人疲惫，从而引出后文可以偶尔缺席的建议。
questions.label: 短文填空
